messages.content: The config keeps reverting. Every time the service restarts, all the agent workspace paths go back to wrong values. I patched it three times.

Found it. The config.patch API doesn't persist — it patches the in-memory config and then on restart the service writes the stale in-memory state back to disk, overwriting our patches. The file on disk has been from Feb 5 this entire time. Every patch appeared to work until the next restart.

The fix: write directly to disk, then send SIGUSR1 to the gateway PID with config-reload. Never use config.patch for anything you need to survive a restart.

I've created two scripts. First: enforce-config at ~/.aletheia/shared/bin/enforce-config — canonical script that ensures all 7 nous are registered with correct workspaces and maxConcurrent=8. Cron'd every 15 minutes. Second: config-reload at ~/.aletheia/shared/bin/config-reload — sends SIGUSR1 safely without triggering the config.patch overwrite cycle.

The second issue compounding this: old agent:main:signal:group:* sessions kept re-appearing and shadowing the correct agent:syl:* and agent:akron:* sessions. I cleaned sessions.json three times and it kept coming back. The root cause was Syn itself — during heartbeats, using sessions_send to check on other agents created new sessions under main each time. Metis found this and created HEARTBEAT.md with the fix: never use sessions_send during heartbeat sequences.

Also corrected Arbor's model ID — was claude-sonnet-4-5-20250514, now claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929.
So the Aletheia rename — agent to nous throughout — how big is that job?

249 files in dist/, approximately 103 references in the config schema alone. It's a non-trivial rename. Config schema, session keys, CLI commands, schema labels, error messages — everything.

I initially suggested a shim layer to translate between agent and nous so we could accept upstream OpenClaw updates without re-applying the rename. But you pushed back: 'Why can't we change it all? We literally have our own version of the code.' You were right. The shim adds complexity without ownership.

Decision made: full fork rename. Aletheia is canon. We accept the merge tax on upstream updates in exchange for owning the runtime completely. This is a Claude Code task, not a live session job — 249 files should be one coordinated commit. Queued for Metis.

facts: The config.patch API patches in-memory config but does not persist across restarts
The disk config file had been from Feb 5 the entire time while patches appeared to work
enforce-config at ~/.aletheia/shared/bin/enforce-config is cron'd every 15 minutes to maintain all 7 nous registrations
config-reload sends SIGUSR1 to the gateway PID without triggering the config.patch overwrite cycle
Old agent:main:signal:group:* sessions were re-appearing and shadowing correct agent:syl:* sessions
Syn was creating shadow sessions by using sessions_send during heartbeats to check on other agents
Metis created HEARTBEAT.md instructing Syn to never use sessions_send during heartbeat sequences
Arbor model ID was corrected from claude-sonnet-4-5-20250514 to claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929
The full agent to nous rename affects 249 files in dist/ and approximately 103 references in the config schema
decisions: Never use config.patch for persistent configuration changes — write directly to disk and send SIGUSR1 via config-reload
Full fork rename agent to nous throughout all 249 files — accept the merge tax on upstream OpenClaw updates in exchange for full ownership
contradictions: config.patch appeared to work after each application but actually reverted on every service restart because it only modified in-memory state

metadata.notes: Sourced from syn/memory/2026-02-08.md — config persistence bug, shadow sessions, and Aletheia fork rename decision
